data.title: Microservices Architecture
data.description: Instagram employs a microservices architecture to handle various functions like user authentication, media processing, and notifications.
data.technical_spec: Microservices typically run on isolated containers, such as Docker, managed with orchestration tools like Kubernetes.

data.title: Scalable Infrastructure
data.description: Instagram's infrastructure is designed for scalability to support billions of users and heavy multimedia content.
data.technical_spec: Utilizes distributed databases and content delivery networks (CDNs) for efficient load balancing and data distribution.

data.title: Event-Driven Architecture
data.description: An event-driven approach enables real-time engagement through notifications, likes, and comments.
data.technical_spec: Relies on message brokers like Apache Kafka to handle data streams and asynchronous processing.

data.title: High Availability
data.description: Designed for minimal downtime and fast recovery to maintain constant uptime for users globally.
data.technical_spec: Employs redundancy, failover systems, and geographically distributed servers.

data.title: Monolithic Frontend
data.description: Despite using microservices at the backend, Instagram's frontend relies on a monolithic architecture for a consistent user interface.
data.technical_spec: Single-page applications built with React that make use of RESTful APIs for backend interaction.
